methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Abridge, a Series E biotech and health company founded by Shiv Rao and Zack Lipton, carries a current post-money valuation of $5.3B on $1.2B+ raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 8 same-sector private peers at similar valuation tiers (Ultima Genomics at $4.0B, Eikon Therapeutics at $3.3B, Insitro at $2.4B, and others) plus 3 public biotech benchmarks (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchored to the $5.3B last post-money applies a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier for Series E advancement, implying $7.4B. Peer-median triangulation across the 8-company comparable set yields $2.5B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.1x) cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures, standard for private companies.

The two methods imply a triangulated range of $2.1B (low) to $8.5B (high) with a median of $5.0B. The current $5.3B valuation sits near the analytical midpoint, supported by stage-progression methodology but substantially above peer medians. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue trajectory and comparable peer growth rates, both critical to justifying the premium over same-sector peers.
